text.title: User Interviews Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every User Interviews application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Every question MUST ask about past behaviour ("when did you last..."), not hypotheticals ("would you...").
rule.rationale: Hypothetical answers are politeness; past behaviour is the only predictor.

rule.statement: Every session MUST end with a behavioural ask (intro / follow-up / prototype access); the yes/no is the data.
rule.rationale: Behavioural ask converts the session itself into a tier-2 commitment signal.

rule.statement: Opener MUST include all 5 steps (vision / framing / weakness / pedestal / ask); the weakness step cannot be skipped.
rule.rationale: Skipping weakness reads as a pitch; respondents perform agreement.

rule.statement: Transcripts MUST be diarized (speaker labels) before signal extraction.
rule.rationale: Un-diarized transcripts mis-attribute interviewer prompts to respondents.

rule.statement: Any 'users want X' claim MUST be supported by a frequency count across ≥3 interviews.
rule.rationale: Single-quote generalisations are confident but worthless.
